race.name: Ford RideLondon
race.tagline: Olympic legacy. 25,000 riders. 100 miles of closed London and Essex roads. The world's greatest festival of cycling — now on indefinite pause after 10 editions and £85 million raised for charity.

vitals.date_specific: Last held: May 26, 2024. Cancelled 2025. Indefinite pause announced February 2026.
vitals.field_size: 25,000+ riders across all distances (20,000+ in the 100-mile alone)
vitals.registration: Historically via public ballot, switched to first-come-first-served in 2024. Standard entry £99 (2024), VIP entry £275. Charity places via fundraising commitments. Not currently open — event on indefinite pause.
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple fully stocked aid stations across the 100-mile route with water, nutrition, and mechanical support
vitals.cutoff_time: Approximately 8.5-9 hours for the 100-mile distance. Roads reopen on schedule.
route_options: 100 miles: Flagship distance — London to Essex loop, start and finish in central London
60 miles: Shortened Essex loop with the same London start and finish
30 miles: Introductory distance through the London boroughs and into Essex
FreeCycle: Free 8-mile traffic-free loop around central London for all ages and abilities

climate.primary: Temperate British late spring — mild but unpredictable
climate.description: Late May in London and Essex typically delivers temperatures between 12-20C (54-68F). The maritime climate means conditions can shift within hours — sunshine giving way to rain and wind without warning. The 2024 edition saw mostly dry conditions, but riders in earlier Surrey-era editions experienced everything from searing heat (2018) to torrential downpours (2017). Essex is flat and exposed, meaning headwinds can be a significant factor on the return leg. May is statistically one of London's driest months, but British weather respects no statistics.
challenges: Rain is always possible in late May — pack a lightweight rain layer
Essex headwinds on exposed flat roads can sap energy on the return leg
Temperature can vary 10C+ between a cool early morning start and midday sunshine
Wet roads in London require extra caution on painted road markings and manhole covers

terrain.primary: Flat to gently rolling closed paved roads
terrain.surface: Entirely paved roads through central London and Essex countryside. The route starts in central London, passes through east London boroughs, enters Essex via Epping Forest, loops through the rolling Essex countryside via towns like Epping, Ongar, and Great Dunmow, then returns through London to finish near The Mall. Road surfaces are generally excellent on the London sections, with some rougher B-road surfaces in the Essex countryside. The Essex route includes a section of the 2014 Tour de France course. Zero off-road, zero gravel, zero technical terrain.
features: Central London iconic landmarks — Tower Bridge, Parliament, The Mall, Canary Wharf
Epping Forest — tree-lined road section marking the transition from London to Essex
Essex countryside loop through Epping, Ongar, and Great Dunmow
Part of the 2014 Tour de France Stage 3 route through Essex
Return through east London boroughs to central London finish

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

course_description.character: A tale of two halves: iconic London landmarks on closed roads bookend a flat, fast loop through the Essex countryside. The experience is the London sections; the miles are the Essex sections.
course_description.signature_challenge: The challenge is not technical or vertical — it is completing 100 miles in the company of 25,000 other riders, navigating the dense peloton safely, and managing your energy across a flat course where the temptation to go too fast too early is constant. The Essex headwinds on the return leg have ended many riders' centuries prematurely.

logistics.airport: Heathrow (LHR), Gatwick (LGW), Stansted (STN), Luton (LTN), London City (LCY) — all within 1-2 hours of central London via rail
logistics.lodging_strategy: London accommodation for event weekend books up fast and is expensive. Budget £150-300+ per night for a central hotel. Premier Inn, Travelodge, and similar budget chains offer better value but sell out early. Airbnb can be cheaper in east London boroughs near the start. Essex-based accommodation (Epping, Chelmsford) is significantly cheaper but requires early transport to the London start. Book as soon as registration confirms.

history.origin_story: Announced in August 2012 by Mayor Boris Johnson alongside double Olympic gold medalist Laura Trott, RideLondon was conceived as a direct legacy of the London 2012 Olympic and Paralympic Games. Managed by London Marathon Events — the same organization behind the London Marathon — it was designed to deliver a world-class cycling festival that would inspire mass participation. The first edition took place on August 4, 2013, with the 100-mile route closely following the 2012 Olympic road race course through London and Surrey, including the iconic climbs of Leith Hill and Box Hill.
notable_moments: 2012: Announced by Mayor Boris Johnson with Laura Trott as Olympic cycling legacy event
2013: Inaugural edition — nearly 16,000 riders complete the RideLondon-Surrey 100 on the Olympic road race course
2013-2019: Annual editions through London and Surrey Hills, featuring Box Hill and Leith Hill climbs
2017: Torrential rain creates treacherous conditions — multiple crashes on the Surrey descents
2018: Heatwave edition — temperatures exceed 30C, causing heat-related withdrawals
2019: Last edition on the original London-Surrey route before COVID-19 pandemic
2022: Returns with entirely new London-Essex route, replacing the Surrey Hills with flat Essex countryside. Rebranded as Ford RideLondon-Essex
2023: Second Essex edition draws 25,000+ riders. Route passes through Epping, Ongar, and Great Dunmow
2024: Final edition held on May 26. Ford RideLondon-Essex 100 entries fail to sell out for the first time — demand has dropped since the route change from Surrey
2024 (September): 2025 edition cancelled. Organisers cite need to 'reimagine the world's greatest festival of cycling'
2026 (February): Placed on 'indefinite pause' following operational and financial considerations. May never return in current form
history.reputation: The world's greatest festival of cycling — a title earned through sheer scale. Over 10 editions, more than 500,000 people participated and more than £85 million was raised for charity. The Surrey-era editions (2013-2019) are remembered fondly for the Olympic road race course. The Essex-era editions (2022-2024) maintained the London magic but lost the dramatic climbs that made the original route special.

biased_opinion.summary: Ford RideLondon was the cycling equivalent of the London Marathon — a mass-participation event where the experience of riding through the capital on closed roads mattered more than your finishing time. Born from the 2012 Olympics, it inspired half a million people to ride and raised £85 million for charity across 10 editions. The move from Surrey to Essex removed the dramatic hills but kept the unique thrill of London's streets without traffic. Now on indefinite pause, it leaves a gap that no other British cycling event can fill.
strengths: Riding through central London on completely closed roads — an experience unavailable on any other day
World-class organization from London Marathon Events with 25,000+ riders managed seamlessly
Massive charity impact: £85 million raised over 10 editions, giving riders purpose beyond personal challenge
Companion UCI Women's World Tour Classique elevated the weekend into a legitimate international cycling festival
Accessible: flat route, multiple distances, and FreeCycle make cycling available to everyone
weaknesses: The Essex route lacks the dramatic climbs (Leith Hill, Box Hill) that defined the original Surrey edition
Flat terrain means minimal physical challenge for experienced riders — this is about distance, not difficulty
Dense peloton of 25,000 riders with varying abilities creates congestion and crash risk
London accommodation costs inflate the total weekend expense significantly
Now on indefinite pause — the event may never return in its current form
biased_opinion.bottom_line: RideLondon was the gateway drug for British cycling participation. If it returns, it will be the most accessible major sportive in Europe. If it does not, British cycling loses something irreplaceable.

final_verdict.one_liner: The world's greatest festival of cycling: 25,000 riders, 100 miles of closed London and Essex roads, and an Olympic legacy that raised £85 million for charity across 10 editions.
final_verdict.should_you_race: If RideLondon returns from its indefinite pause, it is an automatic yes for anyone who wants to ride through central London on closed roads surrounded by 25,000 other cyclists. It is the perfect first century — flat, fully supported, brilliantly organized. It is not an event for riders seeking physical challenge, technical terrain, or competitive racing. It is an event for riders who want an unforgettable experience on two wheels in one of the world's great cities.
final_verdict.alternatives: For UK closed-road sportives: Velothon Wales (closed roads through Welsh valleys). For the original RideLondon climbing experience: Prudential RideLondon Classique route replicas. For European mass-participation road events: L'Etape du Tour (France), Cape Town Cycle Tour (South Africa), Gran Fondo New York.

videos.title: Can We Do An Epic Ride Around London?
videos.description:
London is one of the world’s biggest and most populous cities but certainly not the first place you’d think about when planning the perfect ride. But should it be? Hank met with London native Alec Briggs for a tour of the UK’s capital to find out if you can do an epic ride in a big city!

Watch Cycling Heartlands: London on GCN+ 👉 https://gcn.eu/HeartlandsLDN
Check out the route on Komoot 👉 https://gcn.eu/LondonRoute

Useful Links:
Adventure Documentaries, Exclusive Shows & Live Racing on GCN+: https://gcn.eu/plus

Join the GCN Club: https://www.gcnclub.com/

Where would you choose to go for the perfect city ride? Let us know in the comments below! 💬

If you liked this video, be sure to give it a thumbs up 👍 and share it with your friends!

Photos: © Velo Collection (TDW) / Getty Images & © Bettiniphoto / http://www.bettiniphoto.net/

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside Cycling

videos.title: My RideLondon 2023 report: I need your help!
videos.description:
RideLondon was a fantastic experience, but not everything went well. For my first time I am overall happy, next time I would need to plain it better and improve some aspects.

Let me know in the comments your suggestions!

I’m wearing a kit courtesy of Siroko Cycling, if you want to shop for Siroko kit you can use my direct link for a further 10% discount automatically added to the already reduced prices, no need for other codes. Thank you.

https://srko.co/fede1275

#ridelondon

videos.title: 2023 UCIWWT Ride London Classique - Stage 3
videos.description:
Watch the highlights of the third and final stage of the Ford Ride London, a race part of the 2023 UCI Women’s WorldTour. 

Event Info and Results available at: https://www.uci.org/race-hub/ridelondon-classique-2023/1ggZgQlKsEKnGHXgWrxY8N?tab=stage3-final2
Follow the action on the social media channels:
- Facebook UCI
- Twitter @UCI_WWT
- Instagram @uci_wwt

Discover, join and get your passion rewarded on My World of Cycling, the best cycling platform in the world, at https://myworldofcycling.com

#UCIWWT

videos.title: Tour of Cambridgeshire 2022
videos.description:
After 2 years of cancellations, the Tour of 'Tour of Cambridgeshire finally reappeared and it was an amazing experience. Country Roads closed to cars, flat terrain and hard racing are all the ingredients to a fun day.

Sadly, my Ali Express Garmin/GoPro mount broke early in the race and I could not capture the whole day.

[Music]
foreign
thank you
but just enjoy thank you very much
[Applause]

time

quotes.text: The whole entry area was perfectly organized and we waited up until our load time about 6:15-6:30. It was a bit chilly at the beginning, lots of cyclists everywhere because they were going to let off people in small groups which was maybe the best decision to allow a very spread out race for everyone avoiding big chunk of cyclists gathering up together.

The terrain is not too hilly, it's just a nice rolling road - really the perfect weapon for the event. The bike performed very well, it was unbelievably fast especially at the beginning and I felt extremely strong. Maybe it was the buzz of the event, maybe was the atmosphere.

terrain_notes.text: Nice rolling roads, not too hilly terrain that suits aero bikes well

gear_mentions.text: Small water bottles may not be sufficient - rider ran out of water about 10 miles before the finish and suggests considering larger bottles
53 tooth chainring feels fantastic and gives advantage on downhills when others spin out their gearing

race_day_tips.text: Don't jump into groups too early or push hard at the beginning - be smarter about pacing strategy
Riders are released in small groups which spreads out the field and avoids big chunks of cyclists gathering together
Focus on not crashing - ride aware of everyone around and don't take risks

additional_quotes.text: Set off from Southwest London at five o'clock with three other friends and we rode to the event adding about 10 miles 15 kilometers to the whole Journey
Left the bike outside together with millions of other bikes and came out and the bike got stolen so it was really such a such a sad end for my friend Sam

rider_intel.search_text: Riders describe Ford RideLondon as featuring nice rolling roads that aren't too hilly, making it well-suited for aero bikes. The race organization is described as perfect with ambulances, restoration points, and stewards positioned throughout. Riders are released in small groups to spread out the field and avoid large gatherings. Key challenges include hydration management, as small water bottles may be insufficient with riders running dry about 10 miles before the finish. Pacing strategy is important - riders recommend not jumping into groups too early or pushing too hard at the beginning. The course allows for high speeds on downhills where larger chainrings provide advantages. Safety awareness is emphasized given the mass participation nature. The event atmosphere is described as fantastic with riders enjoying empty roads. Logistics include early morning starts around 5 AM and organized entry areas with timed releases around 6:15-6:30.
